steps.title: Identify the key pattern in the passage
steps.content: The passage describes two opposite outcomes of crab burrowing: in marsh interiors (pannes), burrowing improves drainage and marsh plants recover ✓ good outcome. At marsh edges, burrowing accelerates erosion and causes marsh loss ✗ bad outcome. The blank asks what the burrowing action of crabs “suggests.” The right answer must capture this location-dependent reversal.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — can be beneficial to marshes with small pannes but can be harmful to marshes with large pannes.: ✗ The passage never mentions panne size as a factor — the distinction is location (interior vs. edge), not size.
  • Choice B — may promote increases in marsh plants or decreases in marsh plants, depending on the crabs' location.: ✓ Directly maps interior burrowing (plant increases) to edge burrowing (plant decreases), capturing the location-based contrast.
  • Choice C — tends to be more heavily concentrated in areas of marsh interiors with standing water than at marsh edges.: ✗ The passage compares the effects of burrowing in two locations, not the frequency or concentration of burrowing.
  • Choice D — varies in intensity depending on the size of the panne relative to the size of the surrounding marsh.: ✗ The passage never discusses burrowing intensity or compares panne size to marsh size.
That is the correct answer.
